meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Solve Stuck with Basic Apps
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement professional analytics to overcome the intermediate plateau. Master tracking with Antigravity and Claude Code for your portfolio.
content.answer_block: To break past basic app limitations, you must stop guessing and start measuring. By integrating event-based analytics with Antigravity and Claude Code, you gain granular visibility into user friction points. This data-driven feedback loop transforms your project from a static demo into a professional, scalable product ready for production.
content.intro:
You’ve built the CRUD app, styled the landing page, and pushed the repo to GitHub. Yet, you feel stuck. The code runs, but you have no idea if your users are actually navigating your flow or bouncing at the first sign of complexity. You are building in the dark, and that is exactly why your portfolio projects feel like toys rather than real-world software.

Professional engineering isn't about writing the most code; it's about understanding the user's journey through data. By shifting your focus from 'features' to 'metrics,' you bridge the gap between a student project and a career-defining application. We are going to use Antigravity and Claude Code to wire your app for intelligence, giving you the visibility required to iterate like a senior developer.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the single user action that proves your app provides value. This prevents vanity metric tracking.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase and suggest the 3 most critical user events to track based on my core value proposition.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Identify core user KPIs for this project'

steps.title: Initialize Antigravity SDK
steps.description: Inject the tracking middleware into your application's entry point to capture session metadata without bloating your business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular Antigravity integration pattern for a Next.js 14 environment using standard middleware.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add-dependency @antigravity/sdk && claude run 'Initialize tracking service'

steps.title: Instrument Key User Flows
steps.description: Wrap your primary call-to-action components with event trackers to capture user intent and conversion friction.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable React hook 'useTrackEvent' that wraps Antigravity's track function for consistent analytics logging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create useTrackEvent hook in hooks/ directory'

steps.title: Implement Automated Error Tracking
steps.description: Catch runtime exceptions before your users do. This is the difference between a broken app and a resilient product.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure global error boundary tracking using Antigravity's error logging to capture stack traces and user context.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Wrap root layout in ErrorBoundary and add Antigravity error tracking'

steps.title: Validate Data Pipeline
steps.description: Ensure events are firing correctly in the dev environment before deploying to production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a mock test suite to verify that events are correctly formatted and sent to the Antigravity dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create test file for analytics event validation'

pro_tips: Never track everything. Focus on high-intent conversion events to keep your dashboard clean and actionable.
Use custom user properties to segment your data by user role or subscription tier to identify power users.
Treat your analytics schema like a database schema; document event names and properties in a shared file.
Implement 'Identity' tracking early so you can link user behavior across different devices and sessions.
Set up automated alerts in Antigravity for sudden drops in conversion—it makes you look like a pro when you fix bugs before users report them.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, spend 48 hours observing real user behavior. Identify the point in your app where the most users drop off, then use Claude Code to perform A/B testing on that specific UI component to optimize your conversion rate.
content.conversion_block: You now have the visibility of a senior engineer. Use this data to polish your UI, fix your conversion leaks, and turn your project into a high-performance case study for your portfolio. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
